name: SOL 4h Squeeze Persistence Release v1
edge_mechanism: volatility compresses, a local boundary is challenged, and continuation is entered only if the release persists rather than immediately failing
thesis: Cycle 85 includes volatility squeeze expansion confirmed by persistence instead of first breakout bar. SOL 4h is the better ownership lane than ETH because the live regime is 50/50 chop-transition, which actually gives persistence a chance to exist. The key is to keep the grammar simple enough to trade and avoid the dead sparse squeeze theater that has already failed on other lanes.
invalidation_condition: If SOL 4h squeeze releases still remain too sparse or fail immediately after persistence, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Volatility contracts, a boundary is pressed, the release survives the first test, and price expands directionally.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Many squeeze breaks are fake. Requiring persistence on the release should filter some traps while still entering early enough to catch real expansion in a transition-friendly lane.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h is the best fit because it is the cycle primary lane and has enough transition character to support release persistence better than ETH chop.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The persistence bar may still arrive too late, turning the trade into an exhausted breakout chase.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If count is acceptable but follow-through is weak, take earlier partials before widening the squeeze threshold.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than reclaim ideas, but cleaner continuation payoffs when the squeeze actually resolves.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: SOL 4h clears moderate density and release-persistence entries outperform naive first-break logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated persistence entries that reverse immediately.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable on SOL 4h if the release grammar stays simple

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit primary lane for squeeze release persistence under mixed chop/transition.
test_lanes.continue_if: SOL 4h shows enough persistent releases with acceptable follow-through.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h squeezes undertrade or fail too often after persistence.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a minority of larger expansion legs carrying the result.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same persistence grammar survives the flatter ETH lane even if the fit is weaker.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h still shows some coherent release-persistence behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms persistence needs more transition than pure chop offers.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, lower count persistence releases than SOL.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether stronger trend contamination improves persistence instead of turning it into late chase.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h produces fewer but stronger persistence expansions.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h entries become late trend chase.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger expansion carry than SOL.

long: Next bar low stays >= DONCHIAN_HIGH_20 - 0.12 * ATR_14 and close remains >= EMA_20
short: Next bar high stays <= DONCHIAN_LOW_20 + 0.12 * ATR_14 and close remains <= EMA_20

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 25% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 25% after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR expansion
